title: Tapping for Being the Older Generation
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the moment you realise you are now the adults in the room
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the specific disorientation of becoming the older generation: the moment parents are gone or diminished, and you are now the one others look to. Works through the shock of that shift and toward a grounded acceptance of where you stand.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are aware that the generation above you is gone or diminished, and the shift in your position in the family feels strange or too much.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the disorientation of becoming the older generation. The script works through the specific feeling of standing where the adults used to stand, without anyone above you now, toward a place where that position can be held with some steadiness.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I am now the older generation and I don't quite know how I got here, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though nobody prepared me for standing in this position, I am open to finding my footing here.
Even though I still feel like the younger one in some part of me, I accept that time has moved.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: I am the oldest generation in my family now.
Side of Eye: There is no one above me to turn to in the same way.
Under Eye: I feel too young to be standing here.
Under Nose: The responsibility of this position.
Chin: Missing the people who used to hold this.
Collarbone: The strangeness of being the adult in the room.
Under Arm: Part of me still waiting for someone older to handle it.
Top of Head: How fast the generations have turned.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I have earned this position through decades of living.
Side of Eye: I know things now that I did not know at thirty.
Under Eye: I can be the steady one. I have been the steady one before.
Under Nose: I carry what those before me gave me.
Chin: My experience is the resource in this room.
Collarbone: I can hold this generation with some care.
Under Arm: I am ready to stand here, even when it is hard.
Top of Head: I am the older generation. I can carry that.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
